channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Indiana Walmart
item.description: In an email to FOX59/sent Thursday night, a woman identifying herself as the child's mother said that the monkey's owner showed paperwork for an "emotional support animal.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina sees record turnout for early voting after hurricane
item.description: "The conditions improve each and every day," the executive director of North Carolina's State Board of Elections said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony has a poignant vibe this year
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The Rock & Roll Hall Fame induction ceremony on Saturday night promises to be starry, jamming — and bittersweet. So many of the honorees this time have been lost. Of the seven original members of Kool & the Gang, there is only one, Robert “Kool” Bell.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mississippi couple celebrates 60 years of marriage at Chick-Fil-A
item.description: A Mississippi couple celebrated their 60th marriage anniversary at Chick-fil-A after eating lunch at the restaurant every day for the past two years.
item.dc:creator: Shay Simon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why experts say keeping standard time is 'undeniably' better for us
item.description: Surveys have found that most Americans want to do away with the twice-a-year changing of the clocks. But whether we should observe daylight saving or standard time year round will depend on who you ask.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American despair: In Arizona, high home prices and limited prospects shape voters' presidential pick
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Karl Peterson has been living the cruel inverse of the American dream. His rent keeps getting higher, but his apartments keep getting smaller.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 killed and 8 injured by gunfire following a Mississippi school's football game
item.description: Three people were killed and eight others were injured in central Mississippi when someone opened fire into a group of young people on a trail who were celebrating a school’s homecoming football game.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: The runners-up of this year's contest included a disco party bathroom, a bathroom full of mirrors and neon lights, and a very reflective 80s-inspired restroom in a brunch restaurant, among others.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: Viral sensation 'Lieutenant Dan' arrested for boat being 'public health hazard': police
item.description: A Florida man known as "Lieutenant Dan" who went viral for claiming he was refusing to leave his small home boat ahead of Hurricane Milton has now been arrested for failing to leave a park-side dock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Texas cities represent the divide between those who vote and those who could, but often don't
item.description: Two neighboring Texas cities illustrate the divide in American politics between the diverse, potential electorate in the country and the actual electorate, which is heavily white and wealthier.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What are the squatter laws in Georgia? The Georgia Squatter Reform Act, adverse possession and more
item.description: Georgia is one state that has put forth stronger penalties for squatters, with the Georgia Squatter Reform Act that was signed by Gov. Brian Kemp in April 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: Stanford University says the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With brain injuries a growing problem, the US military tests how to protect troops from blasts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The blast shook the ground and its red flash of fire covered the doorway as U.S. special operations forces blew open a door during a recent training exercise.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia man fatally stabbed inside laundromat for 'no apparent reason': police
item.description: A Philadelphia man was stabbed and killed while playing a game inside a laundromat in an attack police say was carried out for "no apparent reason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — On a desolate slab of island tundra in western Alaska, a resident of Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president, continuing a 12-year tradition for the nation’s westernmost community.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews halt fast-moving brush fire that burned 2 Oakland homes, forced 500 evacuations
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fast-moving fire fed by strong winds burned two homes Friday and damaged several others in a hillside neighborhood in the city of Oakland, where hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate before more than 100 firefighters stopped the progress of the flames, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin US Senate candidates trade accusations of lying during testy debate
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Democratic U.S. Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Republican challenger Eric Hovde repeatedly accused each other of lying over the course of their closely watched Senate race during an often-testy and confrontational debate Friday.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds ordered to evacuate from fast-moving Northern California fire, official says
item.description: At least seven homes were burned by a fast-moving fire in a hillside neighborhood of Oakland, California; hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KTLA 5

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, KRON4

item.title: Big tobacco companies reach tentative multibillion-dollar settlement with Canada
item.description: Three large tobacco companies would pay nearly $24 billion to settle a long-running legal battle in Canada, according to a proposed deal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Video and an unused bullet prove man's guilt in Indiana girls' killings, prosecutor says
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A man charged with killing two teenage girls in a small Indiana community forced them off a hiking trail before cutting their throats, a prosecutor said Friday, telling jurors that the evidence includes an unused bullet and video recorded on the eldest girl’s phone.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas was about to execute Robert Roberson. Then a last-ditch tactic bought him more time
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Robert Roberson, set to be the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome, was nearly out of options to stop his execution in Texas. A state parole board, multiple lower courts and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bodycam shows wild Houston gunfight as officers respond to active home invasion robbery
item.description: Three men arrested after a Houston home invasion escalated into the ambush shooting of a responding officer as intruders held a family at gunpoint.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cheryl Cole asks to give Liam Payne a 'little dignity' after his death
item.description: Cheryl Cole, the ex-partner of Liam Payne and the mother of his son Bear, has broken her silence on the singer's death.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: The fine, white sand helps make Florida's beaches among the best in the world. But the powerful storms have turned the precious commodity into a costly nuisance, with sand creating literal barriers to recovery as homeowners and municipalities dig their way out.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, WFLA, NBC
